name: APITable
tagline: Open-source, API-first low-code database platform — a self-hostable Airtable alternative
metaDescription: APITable is an open-source, API-first low-code platform for building collaborative database apps. Self-host it as an Airtable alternative with Docker.
overview: APITable is an open-source, API-first low-code platform for building collaborative database apps. It pairs a spreadsheet-style interface with a real database underneath, so teams can organize records, link tables, and expose everything through a REST API. The project positions itself as a self-hosted alternative to Airtable.

It is aimed at developers and teams who want a visual database they can host themselves and wire into their own software. Because every datasheet is reachable over the API, you can use APITable as a backend for internal tools, dashboards, and integrations rather than only as an end-user app.

Within the low-code and no-code space, APITable focuses on the database layer: editable grids, multiple view types, cross-table links, permissions, and automation. It is built with TypeScript (Next.js and NestJS) and Java (Spring Boot), and integrates with tools like n8n, Zapier, and Appsmith.
features: Real-time collaboration using an Operational Transformation algorithm, with support for 100k+ rows
Full-stack REST API access from data to metadata, exposed through a one-click API panel
Seven view types including Grid, Gallery, Kanban, Gantt, Calendar, and Mindmap
One-direction and bi-directional table links with infinite cross-links
Row and column permissions, plus folder-level and team/organization access controls
Extensible widget system, automation robots, BI dashboards, and auto-generated forms

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to self-host APITable is the official install script or the all-in-one Docker image. You need Docker and docker-compose v2; 4 CPUs / 8GB RAM or more is recommended.

steps.heading: Install with the official script
steps.body: Run the install script on a host with Docker and docker-compose v2 installed.

steps.heading: Or run the all-in-one Docker image
steps.body: If you prefer a single container, use the all-in-one image. Data is persisted to the local .data directory.

steps.heading: Open the app
steps.body: Once the containers are up, visit the server in your browser and create your first space and datasheet.

steps.heading: Try the hosted version first
steps.body: To evaluate APITable without installing anything, the maintainers also offer a cloud-hosted version and a Gitpod online demo linked from the repository.

useCases: Self-hosting a visual database with a REST API to back internal tools and admin dashboards
Running project, task, or CRM tracking on editable grids with Kanban, Gantt, and calendar views
Embedding datasheets into your own product UI as a configurable data store
Connecting records to automation platforms like n8n, Zapier, or Appsmith for workflow automation
